## The 5 anti-hallucination rules

1. If you don't know it, you don't type it. 2. A fact lives once. 3. Three token types: merge fields, TEAM-TO-ADD links, and "fill after the event." 4. Every date carries its weekday. 5. No result exists before it happens.

## 1. Campaign identity

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| Challenge name | Build Your First AI Marketing Agent Challenge |
| Summit name | The AI Marketing Agent Summit |
| One-line promise | "Build your first AI marketing agent, a fresh content idea every day, in 15 days." |
| Owner | AI School and NAS.com. Host voice: Matt |
| Theme | Market without spending lots of money. Agentic marketing. |

## 2. The agent being built

Every day it researches a fresh, on-trend content idea for the member's business and turns it into a ready-to-run prompt for Claude. Member input: product, customer, niche, 2 to 3 voice samples. Daily output: one idea and one paste-ready prompt. Summit format: 3 experts build 3 agents live (research, image, find-customers).

## 3. Key dates and times (all SGT, 15-day challenge)

| Milestone | Date and weekday | Time (SGT) |
|---|---|---|
| Registration opens | Sun Jun 14 | |
| Doors close | Sat Jun 20 | 11:59pm |
| Day 1, Challenge starts (orientation) | Sun Jun 21 | |
| Day 8, Gate 1 (2-min video) | Sun Jun 28 | deadline 11:59pm |
| Day 14, Gate 2 (final, competition entry) | Sat Jul 4 | deadline 11:59pm |
| Day 15, Graduation | Sun Jul 5 | |
| The AI Marketing Agent Summit | Wed Jul 8 | 11:00pm to 2:00am SGT (3 hrs) |
| Winners announced, credits issued | At Summit (Jul 8), by about Jul 9 | |

## 4. Challenge structure

- 15 days (Sun Jun 21 to Sun Jul 5). Day 1 is orientation. The build runs Days 2 to 14. Day 15 is graduation.
- 6 graded submissions: Days 3, 6, 8, 10, 12, 14. Two gates: Day 8 (video) and Day 14 (final).
- Why 15 and not 14: last time had no separate orientation day. This challenge gives orientation its own Day 1, so first-timers are set up before building starts. The shape is 1 orientation day, 13 build days, 1 graduation day. Same workload, one calm setup day added to lift completion.

## 5. Prize and competition

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| Total pool | $1,000 in Nas.com credits (4 prizes), same structure as the previous challenge |
| Credit type | Nas.com credits, same as last challenge. Issued via Nas.com by about Jul 9. |
| Grand Prize | $500 in credits, best overall agent, brief, and output |
| Runner-Up | $300 in credits, second best overall |
| Honorable Mention | $100 in credits, third, a strong agent |
| Community MVP | $100 in credits, most helpful member during the 15 days (community-voted) |
| HOW TO JOIN | Register at the registration link, free with your NAS.com membership. Open Sun Jun 14 to Sat Jun 20. Challenge starts Sun Jun 21. |
| HOW TO ENTER the competition | Graduate = automatically entered. Your Day 14 submission IS your entry, no separate form. |
| Eligibility | Complete the 6 submissions, pass both gates, be present at the Summit. |
| Judging weights | Agent works 25%, output quality 25%, tailored to business 25%, creativity 15%, community 10%. (Community MVP is voted by the community, separate from the judged prizes.) |
| Must be present to win | Yes |
| WHEN WINNERS are announced | Live at the Summit, Wed Jul 8. A promo email beforehand drives attendance. Each winner is verified as a challenge graduate (Jay) before the slide. |

Note: prize matches the previous challenge exactly. $1,000 in credits across 4 winners ($500, $300, $100, $100). The 4th prize, Community MVP ($100), is voted by the community. Community engagement also counts 10% in the judged prizes.

### 5a. Submission mechanics (the number-one fix from last time)

(1) a link to the working agent or a 60 to 90 sec screen recording, (2) a one-page brief, (3) a demo idea and prompt. Three separate form fields, each takes a link or a file. A team member submits a full dummy entry (Matt and Danny on the entries Drive) before the rules go live.

## 6. Counts and targets

Cohort cap: 300. Summit Zoom capacity: 300. Spots and reg counts are merge fields. Graduation and attendance are filled after the event.

Danny (owner, judging). Matt (host, MC). Alexa (ops). Cholo (tech). Jay (verifies winners are challenge graduates). Support team [ confirm names ]. Three Summit experts, names TBC (placeholders).
